questions.question: A single suction attempt in an adult should generally not exceed:
questions.rationale: Suctioning removes oxygen along with secretions; limit each attempt to about 15 seconds and reoxygenate between passes.

questions.question: When should suction be applied during airway suctioning?
questions.rationale: Insert the catheter without suction; apply suction only on withdrawal to avoid prolonged oxygen removal and mucosal trauma.

questions.question: An oropharyngeal airway is correctly sized from:
options: Corner of the mouth to the earlobe/angle of jaw
questions.rationale: The OPA is measured from the corner of the mouth to the earlobe or angle of the jaw.

questions.question: The chief advantage of two-rescuer BVM ventilation is:
options: A better mask seal and more effective ventilation
It is faster to set up
It avoids the need for adjuncts
questions.rationale: With one rescuer sealing the mask two-handed and another ventilating, the seal and tidal volume delivery improve markedly.

questions.question: A supraglottic airway device functions by:
options: Passing through the cords under direct vision
Sealing over the glottic opening after blind insertion
questions.rationale: Supraglottic (BIAD) devices are inserted blindly and seal over the glottis, allowing ventilation without cord visualization.

questions.question: During trauma intubation, the cervical spine should be managed by:
questions.rationale: Manual in-line stabilization is maintained throughout intubation to protect a potential cervical injury.

questions.question: The most reliable confirmation of endotracheal tube placement is:
questions.rationale: Waveform capnography is the standard for confirming and continuously monitoring tube placement.

questions.question: Nasotracheal intubation is contraindicated when there is suspicion of:
questions.rationale: Nasotracheal intubation (and NPAs) risk intracranial passage with basilar skull fracture and require spontaneous breathing.

questions.question: Why is preoxygenation performed before intubation?
options: To build an oxygen reserve and prolong safe apnea time
questions.rationale: Preoxygenation fills the functional reserve with oxygen, delaying desaturation during the apneic attempt.

questions.question: Before administering a paralytic for RSI, the provider must ensure:
options: The ability to ventilate and a rescue airway plan are in place
questions.rationale: Paralysis removes the patient's own breathing; you must be able to ventilate and have a rescue airway and monitoring ready.

questions.question: External laryngeal manipulation (BURP) improves intubation by:
options: Bringing the glottis into the laryngoscopic view
questions.rationale: BURP/ELM applies backward-upward-rightward pressure on the larynx to optimize the view of the cords.

questions.question: A pulse oximeter reading of 90% on a trauma patient indicates:
options: Hypoxemia requiring supplemental oxygen and reassessment
questions.rationale: An SpO2 of 90% is hypoxemic; provide oxygen, reassess ventilation, and look for a treatable cause.

questions.question: A frequent cause of ineffective bag-valve-mask ventilation is:
questions.rationale: Poor mask seal is the most common reason for inadequate BVM ventilation; correct with two-handed technique and adjuncts.

questions.question: After moving an intubated patient from the ground to the stretcher, you should:
options: Reconfirm tube placement with capnography and breath sounds
questions.rationale: Tube position must be reconfirmed after every move because movement is a common cause of dislodgement.

questions.question: A rigid (Yankauer) suction tip is most useful for:
options: Clearing thick blood and debris from the mouth
questions.rationale: The rigid Yankauer is designed to rapidly clear large volumes of blood, vomitus, and debris from the oropharynx.

questions.question: If a paralyzed patient cannot be intubated or ventilated by BVM or BIAD, the next step is:
questions.rationale: A 'cannot intubate, cannot ventilate' situation requires a surgical airway as the final rescue, per protocol.

questions.question: The OPA is appropriate only when the patient:
questions.rationale: An OPA can trigger gagging/vomiting; it is used only in patients without a gag reflex.

questions.question: Apneic oxygenation during intubation is achieved by:
options: Leaving a nasal cannula running during the attempt
questions.rationale: Oxygen delivered by nasal cannula during laryngoscopy maintains oxygenation and extends safe apnea time.

questions.question: Effective trauma airway management most depends on:
questions.rationale: ITLS stresses that clear communication, role assignment, and preparation drive successful airway outcomes.

questions.question: The mnemonic SOAP-ME is used to:
questions.rationale: SOAP-ME (Suction, Oxygen, Airways, Pharmacology, Monitoring, Equipment) is an intubation preparation checklist.
